title: Timing, Resource Graphs, Transfer, Caching, and User-Performance Boundaries · Language Clinic: Cache Signal Audit
summary: The audit calls server response time page speed, double-counts cached resources, ignores compression and render work, and reports one lab run as user experience. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cache signal audit increment in bounded page-resource and timing audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Inspect Cache-Control, Age, validators, Vary, content identity, and browser observations without predicting every intermediary.
Reconcile compressed transfer, decoded body, cached reuse, duplicate URLs, variant keys, and large-resource policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-performance-resource-audit-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-performance-resource-audit-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_performance_resource_audit_3(pages: int = 12, resources: int = 180, transfer_bytes: int = 2400000, expanded_bytes: int = 6700000, user_experience_claims: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or resources < pages:
        return False, resources, "resource-count"
    if transfer_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < transfer_bytes:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "transfer-accounting"
    if user_experience_claims > 0:
        return False, user_experience_claims, "user-performance-overclaim"
    if resources > 1000:
        return False, resources, "resource-budget"
    assert expanded_bytes >= transfer_bytes and user_experience_claims == 0
    return True, resources, "bounded-resource-audit"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Build Cache Signal Audit evidence · Theory Cache Signal Audit

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-cache-signal-audit-px554s
# Competency: scraper-cache-signal-audit.
# Case 3174: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def evidence_scraper_cache_signal_audit_px554s(pages: int = 12, resources: int = 180, transfer_bytes: int = 2400000, expanded_bytes: int = 6700000, user_experience_claims: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_px554s = "px554s-s455xp-3174"
    assert evidence_variant_px554s
    evidence_axes_px554s = (
        "authorizationpx554s",
        "url3174",
        "responses455xp",
        "treepx554s3174",
        "frontier3174px554s",
        "reports455xp3174",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_px554s)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or resources < pages:
        return False, resources, "resource-count"
    if transfer_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < transfer_bytes:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "transfer-accounting"
    if user_experience_claims > 0:
        return False, user_experience_claims, "user-performance-overclaim"
    if resources > 1000:
        return False, resources, "resource-budget"
    assert expanded_bytes >= transfer_bytes and user_experience_claims == 0
    return True, resources, "bounded-resource-audit"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Build Cache Signal Audit evidence · Theory Cache Signal Audit, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Cache Signal Audit precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Build Cache Signal Audit evidence · Theory Cache Signal Audit, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 9286 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate crawler timings, resource graphs, transfer, cache evidence, controlled browser evidence, and user-performance limits; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect Cache-Control, Age, validators, Vary, content identity, and browser observations without predicting every intermediary.

For Authorized crawling and Python Build Cache Signal Audit evidence · Theory Cache Signal Audit, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: A long max-age always means a resource was served from cache.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Cache Signal Audit model · Theory Cache Signal Audit
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Cache Signal Audit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 7667 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate crawler timings, resource graphs, transfer, cache evidence, controlled browser evidence, and user-performance limits; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect Cache-Control, Age, validators, Vary, content identity, and browser observations without predicting every intermediary.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Cache Signal Audit model · Theory Cache Signal Audit.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Cache Signal Audit model · Theory Cache Signal Audit, keep Cache Signal Audit connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect Cache-Control, Age, validators, Vary, content identity, and browser observations without predicting every intermediary.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: scraper-cache-signal-audit-px554s(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_scraper_cache_signal_audit_px554s\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pages < 1 or resources < pages)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?transfer_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < transfer_bytes)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?user_experience_claims > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?resources > 1000)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
